name: reacTable
organization: Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra
short_description: A round, multi-user tangible tabletop instrument where physical pucks are arranged to patch a modular synthesizer in real time.
long_description: The reacTable is a translucent round table on which performers place and rotate physical objects (tangibles) that represent the modules of a modular synthesizer; their positions, orientations, and proximities dynamically patch and parameterize the audio graph, with the connections and waveforms projected back onto the table surface. A camera beneath the table tracks fiducial markers on the underside of each puck via the reacTIVision computer-vision system, communicated through the TUIO protocol. It is a landmark tangible user interface for collaborative live music, demonstrating how shared, graspable physical objects can make a complex generative system legible and playable by multiple people at once.
ai_note: The system uses fiducial-marker computer vision (reacTIVision) and audio synthesis rules; no machine learning or AI model runs inside it.

images.caption: A reacTable in use, showing tangible pucks and projected synth connections on the round translucent surface (Altman Center, 2007).
images.alt_text: A glowing round translucent table in a dark room with several small physical blocks arranged on its surface and bright projected lines connecting them, with a person's hands interacting.

sources.title: The reacTable: exploring the synergy between live music performance and tabletop tangible interfaces
Reactable - Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra
sources.publisher: Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra

sources.title: reacTIVision: a computer-vision framework for table-based tangible interaction
